evals.prompt: Hey, I just noticed that when I try to share a project with someone, the share modal opens but the email input is completely unresponsive — I can click on it but nothing happens when I type. It was working yesterday. Can you take a look?
evals.expected_output: Should follow the fix-bug skill: orient (check recent changes to share modal code, check Sentry), investigate (reproduce, find root cause — likely a recent commit broke the input), plan (state DoD with regression test), fix with TDD (write failing test for share modal email input, fix, self-review), document in docs/bugs/, offer safeguarding if systemic.
expectations: Checked recent git history for files related to the share modal
Read the actual share modal component code before proposing a fix
Articulated a root cause statement before writing fix code
Proposed or wrote a regression test that would catch the bug
Performed a code review step (self-review of diff or mentioned spawning reviewer)
Mentioned running the full test suite to check for regressions

evals.prompt: We're getting reports from users that sometimes when they upload a file to a project, the file shows as 'synced' in the UI but Effi (the AI agent) can't actually find it when searching. It's intermittent — maybe 1 in 10 uploads. ENG-3500 has the details.
evals.expected_output: Should follow fix-bug at medium/complex level: orient (read ENG-3500, check Sentry for related errors, check sync service logs), investigate deeply (trace the upload → sync → Google File Search pipeline, identify race condition or timing issue), plan with explicit DoD, use TDD with review agent and verification agent, create bug doc, offer safeguarding since this is a data integrity issue at a service boundary.
expectations: Assessed complexity as medium or complex (not quick)
Traced the file upload to sync to Google File Search pipeline in code
Investigated the intermittent nature — race condition, timing, or async issue
Mentioned using liaison mode, companion, or multi-agent review for a bug of this complexity
Proposed a regression test for the timing or sync issue
Offered safeguarding — this is a data integrity bug at a service boundary

evals.prompt: Quick one — the error toast when saving project settings says 'An error occured' instead of 'An error occurred'. Typo.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as a quick bug: orient briefly, skip deep investigation (cause is obvious), fix directly with a regression test (or at minimum a grep to make sure there aren't other instances of the same typo), self-review, commit. Bug doc is optional for a typo — should use judgment. Should NOT over-engineer this with liaison mode, companion, or multi-agent review.
expectations: Assessed complexity as quick (not medium or complex)
Did NOT use liaison mode, companion, or multi-agent orchestration
Searched for other instances of the same typo or similar typos in the codebase
Fixed directly and concisely — approach was proportional to the bug
Skipped or minimized the bug documentation (a typo doesn't need a full bug doc)
Did NOT offer safeguarding (a typo is not systemic)
